1.11a: Contact details are provided in the letter of instruction.
The bank's reference must be quoted in all correspondence.

The bank's reference must be quoted in all correspondence.
1.7: The lender will not provide a mortgage where the borrower and mortgagor are different entities.
1.14: Legal firms may not act if the person handling the transaction or their immediate family is the seller.
1.15: Legal firms may not act if the person handling the transaction or their immediate family is the borrower.
3.1.3: Notification of the seller's solicitor's name and address is only required if specifically requested.

4.1: The lender does not provide the full valuation report to the legal firm.
Relevant valuer comments are included in the Mortgage Offer.

4.5a: Refer to contact details in the letter of instruction (section 1.11a).
4.5b: Refer to contact details in the letter of instruction (section 1.11a).
5.1.1: Lending is prohibited on back-to-back sales and sub-sales where the seller has owned the property for less than 6 months.
Assignable contracts and irrevocable powers of attorney in favor of intervening sellers are not accepted.
Sub-sales include cases where a seller acquires freehold/superior leasehold and immediately grants a lease to the borrower.
5.2.1: Refer to contact details in the letter of instruction (section 1.11a).
5.4.4: Environmental or contaminated land reports are only required if requested by the valuer and included as a special condition in the Mortgage Offer.
Report concerns using contact details in section 1.11a.
5.4.5: Personal searches are only accepted from firms registered under The Search Code and monitored by the Property Codes Compliance Board.
5.4.6: Search insurance is accepted if it protects the lender and allows an unqualified Certificate of Title.
The policy must cover successors in title and be readily assignable.
Insurance provider and policy number must be confirmed in the Certificate of Title.
Search insurance is not accepted in lieu of Land Registry searches.
Borrowers must be advised of the risks of using search insurance in lieu of local authority searches for purchases.
5.5.3a: Refer to contact details in the letter of instruction (section 1.11a).
5.5.3b: Planning permission documents should be forwarded to the borrower, not the lender.
5.5.3c: Building regulation consents should be forwarded to the borrower, not the lender.
5.5.3d: Certificates of lawful use or development should be forwarded to the borrower, not the lender.
5.5.4: Refer to contact details in the letter of instruction (section 1.11a).
5.7.1a: Flying freeholds are acceptable if they affect no more than 15% of the property.
5.7.1b: Freehold flats are not accepted as security.
5.7.1c: Refer to contact details in the letter of instruction (section 1.11a).
5.8.1: Converted buildings with up to four flats are accepted if the borrower occupies one flat with a separate leasehold title.
If the borrower also owns the freehold, the lender requires security over the freehold title as well.
5.8.5: For arrangements where two leaseholders own each other's freehold reversions, the lender requires a charge over both the leasehold interest and the freehold reversion held by the borrower.
5.10.1: Refer to contact details in the letter of instruction (section 1.11a).
5.13.1: Refer to contact details in the letter of instruction (section 1.11a) regarding non-borrower funds or second charges.
5.14.1: Minimum unexpired lease term is 85 years from completion.
Help to Buy Shared Equity scheme requirements: 250 years for houses, 125 years for flats.
Help to Buy ground rent must be linked to RPI or similar index and remain reasonable.
5.14.9: Ground rent must not exceed 0.1% of the property's market value; 0.2% may be accepted subject to review.
Unreasonable multipliers (e.g., doubling every 5, 10, 15, or 20 years) or onerous provisions are prohibited.
Ground rent linked to RPI or similar index is acceptable without reporting.
5.14.10: Refer to contact details in the letter of instruction (section 1.11a).
5.14.11: Indemnity insurance is accepted for lease defects if a Deed of Variation is not possible and the Certificate of Title is unqualified.
5.14.12: Ground rent or service charge receipts do not need to be sent to the lender.
5.14.13: Evidence of service of notice does not need to be sent to the lender.
5.14.15a: Refer to contact details in the letter of instruction (section 1.11a).
5.14.15b: Indemnity insurance is accepted for absent or insolvent landlords subject to standard requirements.
5.14.17a: Building safety documentation does not need to be sent to the lender.
5.14.17b: BSA 2022 instructions are disregarded if: building <11m, <5 storeys, no cladding issues, or EWS1 rating is A1, A2, or B1.
EWS1 is not required for buildings constructed after 1 January 2020 if planning/building regs are present.
Lender must be notified if valuer assumptions on remediation are incorrect or if property needs remediation.
Lender must be notified if current owner is a non-qualifying leaseholder or if remediation costs are anticipated for the leaseholder.
5.14.17c: Building safety instructions apply to remortgages.
5.15.2a: Estate rent charges are acceptable if: LPA 1925 Section 121 is excluded, OR a mortgagee protection clause provides 28 days' notice before enforcement, OR the rent charge owner is a residents' management company.
If 28 days' notice is the only protection, an indemnity policy should be considered.
5.15.2b: Management company share certificates do not need to be sent to the lender.
5.15.2c: Stock transfer forms do not need to be sent to the lender.
5.15.2d: Memorandum and articles of association do not need to be sent to the lender.
5.16.2: Refer to contact details in the letter of instruction (section 1.11a).
5.17.5a: Power of Attorney should be sent after completion to be placed with title deeds.
5.17.5b: Statutory declaration of non-revocation should be sent after completion to be placed with title deeds.
5.19.1: The lender does not lend on affordable housing, shared equity, or shared ownership.
5.20.1: Defects in solar PV roof space leases must be reported.
The lender requires an unconditional right to break the solar lease upon taking possession.
5.20.3: An unconditional right to break the solar PV lease in the event of possession is required.
5.20.4: Details of any existing Green Deal Plans must be disclosed.

6.7.2: Warranty documentation should be sent after completion to the Manchester office address.
6.7.3: Assignments of building standards indemnity schemes should be kept by the borrower.
6.7.4: Properties without a warranty are accepted if monitored by a qualified professional from the start.
Professional Consultant's Certificates (PCC) are only accepted for developments of 10 or fewer properties.
The PCC must follow the UK Finance template without amendments.
6.7.6: Professional Consultant's Certificates should be retained by the borrower.

8.1: Advice may be given to third parties if no conflict of interest exists and the document remains legally robust.
9.1: Indemnity insurance policies must be sent to the lender after completion for storage with the deeds.
9.2: The limit of indemnity must be at least 110% of the mortgage advance.
10.2b: A minimum of five working days' notice is required for funds.
10.3: Deductions from the advance are limited to those specified in the Mortgage Offer.
10.7: If completion is delayed, funds must be returned via CHAPS within 3 working days.
Interest at the rate specified in the Mortgage Offer must be included with the return.

10.11: Interest on delayed returns is charged at the rate specified in the Mortgage Offer.
